## [24:11 - 27:16]

Because the revelation of price is something that you have to do very carefully. And first, you have to build the value before you can reveal price. Now, what I will do sometimes is I won't respond publicly, but I will respond privately. So publicly, I would put something out that basically evades the question that says, so if they've asked, what's it going to cost? Well, first of all, let me step back. The fact that they're asking about the price is a very good thing. It's a very, very good thing. Usually, people don't start asking for the price until, it's usually about 24 to 48 hours before the cart opens, is when they start asking about price. So if they're already asking about price, that's a good thing. And so publicly, I'd respond something along the lines of, we're still finalizing everything with basically just, we're still finalizing everything, dot, dot, dot, we'll let you know soon. Or I'll let you know soon. And then privately, it's a decision. It's what you don't want to do, is you don't want to send someone just, it's going to be $297 or whatever, just because you haven't built a value and they don't even know what they're getting yet. But if it's someone that is really obviously just ready to buy and they just really know what's going on, then you might choose to tell them about it. Then now, one big important thing for you here is this rule of thumb with pricing of, if it's going to be a negative shock, in other words, if when they get to that price, if it's going to be a lot higher than they're expecting, then I will actually reveal the price probably 24 hours ahead of time. And I'll do it like generally in this really, this goes a bit off the script of the standard three videos, but I'll put up like a fourth video that's like a Q&A video right before the open. ## [1:16:06 - 1:19:09]

And frankly, I don't tend to use webinars so much during launches, but I have a lot of students that have done, and they've had great success, and I was thinking about just doing a sort of a surveying the field and seeing what the best practices are. So what I've observed is there are three places to use them, and they're the three places you mentioned. One of them is the night before the launch where you do the webinar and you actually open the cart for a limited period of time and then close it down. I've seen that work really well. The next one is right near the close, like the night of the close, and I've done that before and it was effective, actually I did it. People like to hear about when I screw stuff up, and I had one time it was cart close, and we had our three emails written to go on the day of cart close, and I was actually at a seminar speaking, someone else's seminar speaking that day, so not a good idea to begin with to do the do the close, but I put the emails in and I entered into Aweber, but I didn't actually queue them, so I find out at 5 p.m. that on a closing day we haven't sent a single email, so at that point I called an audible and I said we're going to have an emergency webinar or emergency webinar tonight, and we sent an email to the list that said emergency webinar tonight, and the emergency was I hadn't mailed anything on closing day, so we did a webinar and I just got on into Q&A for like two hours, and that worked it worked real well, although it's one of those it was a closing day, so it's tough to split out, you know, in terms of really know how it did, but in any case that's the second option is to do something like the night of closing, or the day before the night of closing. One of the problems with doing them then is your messaging gets a little squirrely, because you know the day before you close you want to start messaging scarcity about the close, and then the day of close you want to send your three emails and say we're going to close, and you know you got this runaway train going towards the close, and all of a sudden you're not going to throw in some emails that say oh by the way I've got this webinar, so when you're selling something, when you're marketing you can only market one thing, and I'm closing it you want to be marketing a webinar, do you want to be closing, you know, marketing the close, and so that gets a little messy, but I have like I said I've done it, I've seen it done effectively. The third way to do it is in the middle of your cart, and so say if you have a five-day open cart period, which is my favorite five to seven, but let's just say it's a five, or even it's a seven day, and the first day you're basically saying we're open, you know go by, it's awesome, go by, and the second day you're sending them like a social proof email, it's like wow we had such an incredible response yesterday, it was awesome, jump on board, and then the closing day obviously you're going to say we're closing tonight, go by, the day before
